title: The Oil Fields (Joke)
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:the pursuit of oil.}} {{User:WinnDixieSweetTea/Merica Theme}}
[[File:15 and up.png|right|frameless|200px|link=]] '''Content Warning'''
This page contains sensitive content '''not suitable for some audiences'''. Readers including you might consider these topics anxiety inducing: '''Depictions of gun violence''', '''blunt political commentary''', '''themes of trauma''', and '''non-graphic death'''. As the author, I do '''NOT''' condone the attempted assassination of politicians. Also, as a work of fiction, it is not meant to directly refer to any real life events. Any semblance to a real life tragedy, war, or otherwise is completely unintentional.
'''This is an American joke page.'''
''This page is American; it is not meant to be taken seriously, and is only for American laughs.''
----
'''☆ ☆ ☆'''
Fire. Everywhere I looked, there was fire. The screams from the many innocent researchers and people who had once lived here echoed for a brief moment before being abruptly cut out by more explosions. This place was safe just a few days ago, a small community of wanderers where everyone knew each other. Now, it was a hell hole where the only escape was death. But I didn't want to die. For the next several hours I walked, because I knew if I stopped walking, I'd be seen, and if I was seen, I'd die. This mentality didn't last for long, and I eventually collapsed. I looked up, and next thing I knew, a man was walking in my direction. Using the minimal amount of strength I had left, I choked up one word: "Help." The man knelt down and looked at me for a few seconds before taking off his visor. "American Liberation Forces, you need help?" I stammered a few more words: "Why do these people have to die?" With a scornful expression, he said "It's war; death is expected. Another civilian death is just another bump in the road." Before I had the chance to respond, he walked away at a brisk pace. I was pissed. Out of spite, I closed my eyes and dug my fingers in the ground. Suddenly, I felt something cold and viscous. I opened my eyes, and realized the liquid was oil. I stared at my hands that were now stained with the substance, the lifeblood of the economy. That's when everything clicked: all of this — the death, the devastation, the fire — was because they wanted oil.
'''☆ ☆ ☆'''
 ---- {{Audio|align=center|volume=0.2|loop=yes|mono=yes|file=Pumpjack.mp3}} {{Class Custom Image|hexagon=🇺🇸|class=3e - Environmental|safety=High Pollutant Rates|security=Increasing Instability|entity=Minimal Entity Count|bg-image=Waving US Flag.gif}} '''The Oil Field''' is an anomalous level in the Backrooms known for its high abundance of oil, hence its name. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Oil Fields.jpg|caption=One of the Oil Field's most heavily industrialized regions.}} '''The Oil Field''''s mainland is a desert that expands in all directions, encompassing a total land mass of 15,000 sq mi, similar in size to Maryland. The desert does not significantly differ in appearance from a desert found in [[The Frontrooms|baseline reality]] in its typical state. As of present, however, the '''Oil Field''' is heavily industrialized and under the presence of many human inhabitants, primarily from the New America. Despite the level's industrialized state, minimal formal urban infrastructure exists other than pumpjacks or construction sites. Consequently, most wanderers and inhabitants travel between areas by foot or by soliciting rides in military vehicles. The relative flatness of the '''Oil Field''' has enabled the rapid and significant building of infrastructure; the highest documented peak on land is only 655 ft.This is more than double the height of the Statue of Liberty. However, in a topographical context, a peak of 655 ft is very flat. Plant life is near lacking, most of which has been killed off by the aggressive pursuit of building, with the only persisting specimens existing in the '''Oil Field''''s farthest reaches. These work in tandem to create detrimental effects on its weather cycle by polarizing daytime and nighttime temperatures, and significantly lowering humidity levels on land. Residents of the '''Oil Field''' live in coastal areas or in shaded living spaces to mitigate these effects. {{Blurimage|file=Pumpjacks.gif|caption=Pumpjacks in the Oil Field work tirelessly to extract oil.|align=left}} Perhaps the most notable aspect of the '''Oil Field''' — and its namesake — is its significant oil presence, containing more oil than most others Backrooms levels.Considered to be tied with [[Level 389]], whose oil acropolis has an infinite amount of oil. Oil saturation within the ground often exceeds 70% with high porosity and an average reservoir thickness of 70 ft.This is roughly 140 hot dogs deep. The soil's oil-rich properties evidently leads to an extremely high oil output, estimated at roughly 30,000 barrels recovered per acre. The high abundance of oil and its connection to [[‘Merica_(Joke)|'Merica]] has made it attractive for many groups since the oil's discovery. However, a sizeable portion of the '''Oil Field''''s is allotted to one group, ultimately hindering the actual output of its oil across the wider Backrooms. While the '''Oil Field''''s temperatures are extreme and unpredictable, researchers have devised seasons based on other weather phenomena. It is standardized into three seasons: active, intermediate, and inactive, with the most recent season lasting 14 months in FD time. They are primarily derived from wind speed and dust storms; active features thick, dangerous dust storms and wind speeds as high as 30 mph, while inactive features little to no wind speeds or dust storms. Intermediate is between both seasons. The intensive extraction and refinement of oil within the '''Oil Field''' is recorded to be slowing down the season cycle due to the emission of greenhouse gases. It is believed that by 2060, the season cycle will cease completely and extremify the existing wind speeds and dust storms present in the active season. {{Blurimage|file=Offshore Drill.jpg|caption=The Oil Field's ocean, showing two oil rigs.}} Beyond the '''Oil Field''''s mainland exists an immeasurably large ocean. While its oil presence is generally more inconsistent, certain areas in the seabed can have a much higher oil saturation and reservoir thickness than on land. This makes the ocean very enticing for groups — such as the New America — willing to maximize oil output. Similar to the mainland, the ocean has a substantial amount of oil-mining infrastructure, with oil rigs being placed relatively close together. Despite being safe to touch and drink initially, the various cost-cutting measures put in place when building oil rigs have led to frequent oil spills. Because of this, even short-term exposure to the water can lead to redness and swelling, and consumption is impossible without intensive filtering. ==Entities== While the '''Oil Field''' may have had a larger entity population in the past, the abrasive colonization and industrialization have killed off a major portion of it. Despite this, reports of common entities — such as [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], [[Entity 6|Dullers]], and [[Entity 8|Hounds]] — are scarcely made in far-flung areas. There have also been instances of [[Entity 5|Clumps]] hiding in tight spaces in machinery. It is projected that if the rate of pollution in the '''Oil Field''' continues, the remaining entity population will be killed off entirely within the next ten years. ==Colonies & Outposts== '''The Oil Field''' has historically been attractive to many settlers due to its high oil abundance, but one group exercises far more control than others. ===The New America=== {{Blurimage|file=New America.png|caption=Flag used by the New America.}} The New America is by far the most influential group in the '''Oil Field''', proclaiming itself as the direct Backrooms continuation of the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States United States]. Originating from a previous iteration of [[‘Merica_(Joke)|'Merica]] in 1974, it was founded by former Maryland senator Daniel Brewster after noclipping two years prior. After silently building their military and technology over the next two decades,In 1994, the New America had roughly 4,500 soldiers in active service, nearly two-thirds of its civilian population. it was eventually drawn to the '''Oil Field''' in 1997, engaging in a violet military altercation that lasted two weeks. Boasting a population of 15,000 residents and 2,000 officials, it has the highest population of any group in the level. It is also responsible for the rapid industrialization of the '''Oil Field''' and constitutes between 85–90% of its oil production. While their '''Oil Field''' colony is their largest by a wide margin, the New America maintains a notable presence in [[Florida (Joke)|Florida]] and [[‘Merica_(Joke)|'Merica]]. The New America denies any affiliation with the group [[Level 389#State 51|State 51]], despite their similar American values. ===B.E.G. "Mojave" Outpost †=== The "Mojave" Outpost was the dominant outpost prior to the establishment of the New America colony, founded in 1988 by the Burger Endurance Group. Its population was estimated to be between 1,000 and 1,500. After oil was discovered by a B.E.G. research team in 1997, the following altercation that occurred with the New America nearly wiped out the outpost in its entirety, with an estimated population of 200 in 1998. Eventually, the fierce competition with the New America afterward forced the disestablishment of the outpost in 2000. ==Entrances & Exits== The primary method of entering the '''Oil Field''' is noclipping through a poster advertising a "desert vacation." While these posters can be found anywhere in the Backrooms, they are more easily found in levels loosely related to the theme of Americana. The methods of exiting are more varied. One can access a manhole in order to enter [[Level 389]], noclipping in designated "hotspots"Hotspots are certain areas in the '''Oil Field''' where the [[Achospic Plane]] is more unstable. can lead to [[‘Merica_(Joke)|'Merica]], and rare, intermittent noclips could send one to random levels. ==Footnotes== ----
'''☆ ☆ ☆'''
It has been three months since the warring ended. In just three months, I've only grown more and more upset with what this place has become. Everywhere I looked — the places that used to be laboratories and homes — were now being wrecked and replaced by these ridiculous pumpjacks. The dozens of people who were forced out of safety into their own demises were brushed aside in the grand pursuit of ''black gold'', as they had called it. However, everything about it was nonsense. It can't ''just'' be the oil, they must simply want power. Because they're tied to a nation from the Frontrooms, they think they can just do shit, and everyone buys into it because they have no idea how to fend for themselves; something I had been doing for years in this horrible dimension. It wasn't even up to the people, either. After Brewster stepped down in 1980, Turner completely dissolved the election system. I wish there was a way to finally prove to those helpless people they themselves could do something. It just couldn't go on. I thought, and then finally remembered: The speech. It's happening today. I contemplated everything that led up to this moment. ''Do I have to really do this? Is it worth it?'' I was pacing in my complex quickly and shakily. I thought about my friends. How I watched as they were shot, how they did nothing, yet they were punished. I thought about the fire. There was a calling for me — I don't know from who — to finally bring back justice after being deprived of it. And I was going to do exactly that. I punched in the number to the safe and took out my revolver. I looked in the cylinder; it's all just casings. I scrambled around in the safe and managed to find three bullets. ''Damn it,'' I thought, ''that's barely enough!'' As fast as I could, I tore out the cylinder to put in what few bullets I had and slapped the ejector rod as hard as possible, scattering casings all over the floor. Didn't matter where they went, I probably wouldn't see this room again. I flicked the gun shut with my wrist and left as soon as I could.
'''☆ ☆ ☆'''
I was left tempting my own patience to kill. As I held the revolver to my side, I stood carefully yet I was trembling. The man in front of me was the "president" of this horrid bureaucracy, Lewis Turner. As I listened, he claimed to bear "the torch of democracy," speaking confidently to an audience who believed his lies. ''But then why would you '''murder''' my colleagues over a natural resource?'' I clenched the gun harder. ''Why are you '''polluting''' this level into hell?'' I stared aptly with nothing but rage. I couldn't tolerate it anymore. It was everything he was doing. All these lies weaponized against innocent people, the destruction of this level's balance. I brandished my revolver and aimed it directly at Turner, and shot. {{Audio|align=center|volume=1|loop=no|mono=yes|file=bangbang.mp3}} I missed both times. My attempt to finally bring back the safety of this place was overturned. A man walked up to me and seized my gun, and I reached out for it. I was kicked the ground several times. My face was bruised and bleeding. I didn't have the chance to get up, and the man wielded his baton. I was knocked cold.
'''☆ ☆ ☆'''
Immediately after waking up, it was clear I was no longer out in the field. It was a small concrete room maybe fifteen feet large in both dimensions. The walls were cracked and water was dripping from the ceiling — probably an old room that hadn't been taken care of in years. In the corner of the room was a dusty, old television on a stand. I tried to stand up from the chair I was in before I had realized my arms and legs were both tightly bound to it, I couldn't move. ''What the hell?'' I didn't know what to expect. Greeting me was the door in the corner of the room creaking open, revealing a suited man walking in. He was tall and slender — easily six foot — who walked with a unnaturally straight back. He stood in front of me and let out a disconcerting smirk. Firmly, he put his hand on my shoulder and sighed. "You are messed up in your head. You know that?" He lifted his hand.
"I could just as easily say the same about you."
"I didn't try to kill our President. If I was crazy, I wouldn't even scratch the surface of your level." The longer I stayed bound to my chair the more pissed I got. The more impatient I was. Whatever they were planning on doing to me — considering where I was — it was ''not'' going to be good. I grimaced and grunted, struggling to maintain a good posture.
"Whatever, why am I even here?" "We want you to admit what you did, and pledge your allegiance to the New America," he responded. "Excuse me? Absolutely not." As I spoke, he pulled out a bulky camcorder from the TV stand and input a tape. "I was expected that response," he muttered, preparing to record. He stared at me blankly and asked "what was your motive?". "I wanted to kill your President. He is a ''horrible'' man. Turner was responsible for the death of hundreds of innocent wanderers and the destruction of this once peaceful level's environment!" "Kid, you want to know something?" His eyebrows furrowed. "We're not stupid. We know that. The thing is that you need to check the logistics. Barrels are worth more than lives, and it fuels the war effort, which makes us powerful." Standing there, he awaited a response. I mustered out "So? Even if it isn't today, in the future, your little façade will fall apart. A new generation of cleaner, brighter eyes will succeed you." He snickered. "That's just you. It's been proved for ''decades'' that it is easier and more effective to make people afraid of you than to make them love you. It guarantees obedience and provides order to an otherwise disorganized world. People like you are just bumps in the road, pests that need to be crushed. And that's why—" he pulled out the very gun I used against Turner "—you were brought here." "It's ironic, isn't it? A man trying to break the monotony of the system, only for it to fight back and exterminate him." I didn't want to admit it in the moment, but he wasn't wrong. I failed to do the one thing I had on my mind, the thing I had conspired for hours beforehand, kill Turner. "Let's see what your man is talking about." He went over to the stand and turned on the television, revealing that my death was going to be broadcasted. My stomach sank. "...and justice for all!" Everyone cheered for Turner. The man walked up to me, pulling a lavalier microphone from his collar, and spoke. "You are now watching the televised death of Christopher Smith. Take this as a warning to those who disobey the great New America." I was calming down, trying to breathe, knowing being tense was going to do nothing. He lifted the gun to my head and said "don't worry, we're going to keep that," lowering it down to my heart. Turner continued to speak. "Obedience brings order to an inherently evil, destructive world. It can be traced back to the story of the very first humans. They disobeyed their god, and they were given a cruel punishment for it. In this case — right now — I am your god. If ''anyone'' dares to disrespect my name or the New America, I will ensure they get the cruelest punishment possible. Any form of disobedience deserves death. And if ''all'' of you choose to disobey me? You will all die. Order is important, and I must step in to ensure it isn't broken. Hail the New America! God let us live on!" In my final moments – despite my horror — I felt hope. Not hope for myself or anyone in particular, but for the future. The requiem for dissent has been held for centuries, but time and time again, the repetition of it has been broken. In ten, maybe twenty, maybe thirty years, people will look back and think of the torture their ancestors endured. My death will not be for naught, and instead be a mere ember before the fire. Finally, he shot me. My blood is beginning to run cold. However, I'm still smiling because I know better than him. The New America's paper wings will burn. [[File:Burning flag.png|300px|center|borderless|link=]]
'''☆ ☆ ☆'''
 ---- {{LinkCollapse|initial='''> Open Author & Credits'''|final='''> Close Author & Credits'''|body=This is my first actually finished piece of writing in over two years! I wanted to write something more than just a generic article, but knew that I couldn't execute anything in a serious format either. This is supposed to blur the lines between mocking "free" governments while also including gritty political commentary. Because this is my first attempt at actual narrative writing, this is definitely not great, but I'm really proud of it. The narrative section of this page is ~1,700 words, so it's technically a short story!! The entire page is ~2,800 words.
'''Author:''' [[User:WinnDixieSweetTea|WinnDixie]]
